raw_httpx.title: Højgaard Installation | Din El-installatør i hele Trekantsområdet

findings.description: Missing HSTS header (HTTP Strict Transport Security)
findings.risk: Browsers are not instructed to always use HTTPS. On unsecured networks (public WiFi), a visitor's first connection could be intercepted before the redirect to HTTPS occurs.

findings.description: Missing Content-Security-Policy header
findings.risk: The browser has no restrictions on which scripts can run on the page. If the site is compromised, injected scripts can operate without constraint.

findings.description: Missing X-Frame-Options header
findings.risk: The website can be embedded in frames on other sites. This enables clickjacking attacks where users interact with hidden elements overlaid on the legitimate page.

findings.description: Missing Permissions-Policy header
findings.risk: The browser is not instructed to restrict access to device features (camera, microphone, geolocation). Third-party scripts embedded on the page could request these permissions.

findings.description: Missing Referrer-Policy header
findings.risk: The browser may send the full page URL to third-party sites when visitors click links. This can leak internal page paths or query parameters.

findings.description: WordPress version 6.9.4 publicly disclosed
findings.risk: The exact WordPress version is visible to anyone viewing the page source. This allows attackers to look up known vulnerabilities specific to this version and target them directly.

findings.description: 8 WordPress plugins detected
findings.risk: Each plugin is additional code from a third-party developer. Outdated or abandoned plugins are a common entry point for attackers. Plugins should be reviewed and kept updated.

findings.description: Backend technology exposed: MySQL, PHP
findings.risk: The server advertises which backend technologies it runs. This gives attackers information about which exploits may be applicable. It does not mean the site is vulnerable, but it reduces the effort needed to find an attack vector.

findings.description: WordPress theme detected: Hoejgaardny
findings.risk: The active theme is visible in the HTML source. Outdated themes are a common source of vulnerabilities, similar to plugins.

findings.description: Outdated plugin: Cookie Notice (installed 2.0.4, latest 2.5.16)
findings.risk: The installed version of Cookie Notice is behind the current release. Outdated plugins may contain known vulnerabilities that are fixed in newer versions.

findings.description: Outdated plugin: Ultimate Member (installed 2.8.2, latest 2.11.3)
findings.risk: The installed version of Ultimate Member is behind the current release. Outdated plugins may contain known vulnerabilities that are fixed in newer versions.

findings.description: Outdated plugin: Elementor (installed 3.1.1, latest 4.0.1)
findings.risk: The installed version of Elementor is behind the current release. Outdated plugins may contain known vulnerabilities that are fixed in newer versions.

findings.description: Outdated plugin: Wordpress Seo (installed 16.2, latest 27.3)
findings.risk: The installed version of Wordpress Seo is behind the current release. Outdated plugins may contain known vulnerabilities that are fixed in newer versions.
